calendar.matters:
Order To Show Cause Re: Why Respondent Shall Not Be Held In Comtept For Failure To Comply With The Writ
Off Calendar Mar-15-2022 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES:
Ansrew Zacks, 415.956.8100 of Zacks, Freedman & Patterson, PC appearing vis Zoom for Petitioner Peter Ownes.
Brian Crossman, 415.554.4690, Deputy City Attorney for the City and County of San Francisco appearing via Zoom for Respondent City and County of San Francisco.
Reeported: Raquel Sharp, CSR 10619, 925.384.0003, Raquel@transcriptlegal.com.

Argued. (See Reporter's transcript for full details) The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows: HEARING ON ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE RE: CONTEMPT is DENIED. The writ issued by this Court directed the respondents to "set aside your decision denying Petitioners' Application and approve that Application forthwith." "That Application" referenced in the writ was approved on May 17, 2021. Contrary to Petitioner's reading of the Court of Appeal's directions and this Court's Order on the Writ of Mandate the directive "to conduct any other proceeding consistent with the views expressed in this Court of Appeal's opinion" were addressed to this Court (i.e. "directing this Court to (1) grant and (2) to conduct." No argument is made and no argument is provided that there was any delay in the approval of the application by the Department of Public Works on May 17, 2021, about 20 days after the service of the writ of mandate. Prevailing party to prepare order in compliance with CRC 3.1312(b). Judge: Charles F. Haines; Clerk: J. Murphy; Reported: Raquel Sharp, CSR 10619, 925.384.0003, Raquel@transcriptlegal.com. (D501)

Petitioners' Application For Enforcement Of Writ Of Mandate And Issuance Of Order To Show Cause Re: Contempt
Off Calendar Jan-24-2022 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES"
Emily Brough, 415.956.8100 of Zacks, Freedman & Patterson, PC appearing va Zoom for Plaintiffs Peter Owens et al.
No other appearances.

Not argued. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows: PETITIONERS' APPLICATION FOR ENFORCEMENT OF WRIT OF MANDATE AND ISSUANCE OF ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE RE: CONTEMPT is GRANTED. A hearing on order to show cause why respondent shall not be held in contempt for failure to comply with the writ is set for March 15, 2022. Any response shall be filed and served no later than February 17, 2022. Any reply shall be filed and served no later than March 3, 2022. Proposed order submitted for review. Judge: Charles F. Haines; Clerk: J. Murphy; Not Reported. (D501)
